title: Rise and Fall of Civilizations
whatItIs:
This topic studies the recurring pattern of civilisations expanding, organising, enriching themselves, then becoming fragile through overreach, conflict, stagnation, or environmental strain.

It is not a prophecy. It is a way to compare historical cases and notice the conditions under which success becomes vulnerability.
whyItMatters:
It matters because every modern society likes to believe its current arrangement is permanent. History keeps showing that no coordination system is exempt from entropy, conflict, or complacency.

The lens helps you ask which parts of a civilisation are genuinely resilient and which are merely impressive while conditions are favourable.
keyIdeas: Overreach and complacency are recurring risks
frontier: The frontier question is whether modern global civilisation can avoid repeating old collapse patterns at planetary scale. That makes governance, resilience, and shared truth infrastructure unusually important.
openQuestions: Can collapse be partial rather than total?
What modern systems most resemble old imperial fragility?
